title: Google says its AI systems helped deter Play Store malware in 2025
description: Google said it prevented 1.75 million bad apps from going live on Google Play during 2025, a figure that's down from previous years.

title: An AI data center boom is fueling Redwood’s energy storage business
description: Redwood Materials says its new energy storage business is the fastest growing unit within the company.

title: Why these startup CEOs don’t think AI will replace human roles
description: The CEOs of Read AI and Lucidya told TechCrunch at Web Summit Qatar that they see AI tools replacing tasks, rather than workers.

title: YouTube’s latest experiment brings its conversational AI tool to TVs
description: YouTube is testing conversational AI on smart TVs, allowing viewers to ask the assistant questions related to the video they're watching on the big screen.

title: The boys’ club no one was supposed to write about
description: Reporter Zoë Bernard spent months talking to 51 people (31 of them gay men) to map out a subculture that's been an open secret in Silicon Valley for years: gay men, at the upper echelons of tech, quietly raising up their own networks the way powerful people have always done.

title: Reddit is testing a new AI search feature for shopping
description: A small group of users in the U.S. will start to see search results that include interactive product carousels with pricing, images, and direct where-to-buy links.

title: Zuckerberg grilled in court over social media harms on teens
description: Meta's CEO was questioned over the addictive nature of its social media apps like Instagram, and other teen harms.

title: OpenAI reportedly finalizing $100B deal at more than $850B valuation
description: OpenAI is reportedly getting close to closing a $100 billion deal, with backers including Amazon, Nvidia, SoftBank, and Microsoft. The deal would value the ChatGPT-maker at $850 billion.

title: Current is a new RSS reader that’s more like a river than an inbox
description: Current introduces a stress-free, totally reimagined RSS news reading app as a one-time paid download.

title: Sex toys maker Tenga says hacker stole customer information
description: The Japanese sex toy maker said a hacker broke into an employee's inbox and stole customer names, email addresses, and correspondence, including order details and customer service inquiries.

title: Reload wants to give your AI agents a shared memory
description: Reload announces a $2.275 million raise in a round led by Anthemis and the launch of its first AI employee, Epic.

title: Rivian owners will soon be able to access vehicle controls using their Apple Watch
description: Rivian is launching a companion app that pairs with Apple Watch in the coming week.

title: The AI security nightmare is here and it looks suspiciously like lobster
Apple TV’s The Hunt finally has a premiere date
West Virginia sues Apple for allegedly letting child abuse spread in iCloud
